conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children for whom you'd like to claim Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have a child, Alex, who's 10 years old. I'm wondering if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! And do you live with Alex, or do they live with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex doesn't live with me - they live with someone else.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I understand. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to ask: do you contribute towards Alex's upkeep? For example, do you regularly provide money for things like food, clothes, pocket money, or contribute financially towards their accommodation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards Alex's upkeep. I help out with things like clothes and other bits and pieces.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To determine your eligibility, I need to know more about the amount you're contributing. 

Do you contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment towards Alex's upkeep? For reference, Child Benefit is currently £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child (approximately £110 per month). This can include things like food, clothes, pocket money, or financial contributions towards their accommodation.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, to be honest, I contribute about £20 a week towards their upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for being honest about that. I have one more important question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex based on what you've told me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex** at this time.

Here's why: While Alex is 10 years old (under 16) and you live in the UK, Alex doesn't live with you. According to the rules, to be considered responsible for a child who doesn't live with you, you must contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment towards their upkeep. 

The current Child Benefit rate for an only or eldest child is £25.60 per week. You're contributing £20 per week, which is less than this amount. Therefore, you don't meet the requirement to be considered responsible for Alex under the Child Benefit rules.

**However**, if you were to increase your contributions to at least £25.60 per week (or the equivalent value in things like food, clothes, pocket money, or contributions towards accommodation), you could potentially become eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, as long as no one else claims for them.

If you have any questions or if your circumstances change, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for further guidance.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 10 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if either: (1) you live with them, or (2) you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them - for example on food, clothes or pocket money. In this case: Alex is 10 years old (under 16) and you live in the UK, which meets the basic criteria. However, Alex does not live with you. You contribute £20 per week towards Alex's upkeep, but the Child Benefit payment for an only/eldest child is £25.60 per week. Since you are contributing less than the Child Benefit amount, you do not meet the 'responsible for a child' test under the rules. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and has a 10-year-old child (Alex) who does not live with them. While the claimant contributes £20 per week towards Alex's upkeep, this is less than the Child Benefit payment amount of £25.60 per week for an only/eldest child. To be considered responsible for a child who doesn't live with you, you must pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards their upkeep. Since the contribution falls short of this threshold, the claimant does not meet the eligibility criteria.

While the claimant contributes £20 per week towards Alex's upkeep, this is less than the Child Benefit payment amount of £25.60 per week for an only/eldest child. To be considered responsible for a child who doesn't live with you, you must pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards their upkeep. Since the contribution falls short of this threshold, the claimant does not meet the eligibility criteria.
